title: Digital Connection vs. Mental Well-being
content: Title slide introducing the presentation topic on digital connection and mental well-being. Presented by Li Wei, a Year 2 Sociology student, for Global Studies 201.

title: The Ubiquitous Landscape
content: This slide establishes Gen Z as 'Digital Natives' who experience life through an online lens. It provides usage statistics for popular platforms and frames constant connectivity as an indispensable part of modern life.
key_claims: 70% of students engage with Instagram and Snapchat
Constant connectivity is a fundamental and indispensable reality for Gen Z

title: Usage and Anxiety Statistics
content: This slide presents a bar chart correlating heavy digital usage with negative mental health outcomes, citing a 2025 Mental Health Statistics Report. It shows the percentage of users exceeding 2 hours of daily usage, those reporting anxiety/sadness, and an increased depression risk factor.
key_claims: 87% of users spend more than 2 hours per day online
There is a 70% increase in depression risk factor

title: Psychological Manifestations
content: This slide outlines three key psychological effects stemming from digital media use: FOMO (Fear of Missing Out), social comparison, and cyberbullying. Each effect is explained with a brief description of its impact on mental health.
key_claims: FOMO effect creates significant stress and anxiety regarding social inclusion
Constant comparison via curated feeds leads to negative self-image and low self-esteem
Nearly 87% of teens have experienced some form of online harassment or negative interactions

title: Strategic Mitigation Efforts
content: This slide proposes three strategies to address the negative mental health impacts of digital media: digital detox, increased awareness, and platform responsibility. It emphasizes the shared responsibility between individuals and companies in fostering emotional balance.
key_claims: Implementing time limits (digital detox) is a crucial strategy for emotional balance
Education regarding platform algorithms is essentially significant
Companies should prioritize user well-being as a primary imperative
